summary: Backend-only verification of iteration 167 changes: (1) new smart-report periods (yesterday/last_month/six_months/year) for /api/smart-reports/sales and /api/smart-reports/products; (2) cashier_id filter on /api/reports/expenses; (3) _apply_business_date_filter refactor across 9 endpoints in routes/reports_routes.py; (4) migration idempotency. Created /app/backend/tests/test_smart_reports_periods_iter167.py with 36 pytest cases. Result: 36/36 PASSED. All new smart-report periods return 200 with consistent dict payload (total_sales, total_orders). six_months window is monotonic >= month window. Expenses cashier_id filter correctly narrows results (every returned row has created_by==cashier_id; nonexistent id returns empty list and total_expenses=0). All 9 /api/reports/* endpoints accept start_date/end_date and return 200 (no MongoDB $or/$and conflict from refactor). Migration endpoint is idempotent: second run returns shifts_updated=0, orders_updated=0, expenses_updated=0.

minor.issue: These endpoints declare start_date and end_date as REQUIRED query parameters (no default); calling them without dates returns 422. Other reports (sales, purchases, products, profit-loss, delivery-credits, expenses) make them optional. Inconsistent UX — frontend must always pass dates for these 4. Not a regression from this iteration but worth reconciling.

period='custom' branch only sets start when start_date is provided; otherwise silently falls through to the else branch (today). If the user sends period=custom with only end_date, end_date is ignored. Edge case — low impact.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Make start_date/end_date optional with defaults for /api/reports/cancellations|discounts|credit|card to match other reports (currently 422 without dates).
OPTIONAL: Tighten period='custom' handling in /api/smart-reports/sales to honour end_date alone, not just start_date.
critical_code_review_comments: _apply_business_date_filter helper (routes/reports_routes.py line 21-46) is correctly defensive: detects existing $or in the query and merges via $and to avoid the documented 'lazy MongoDB $or conflict'. Verified by hitting all 9 endpoints with date-range params — no 500s.

GET /api/reports/expenses (line 334-402) correctly applies cashier_id as query['created_by']=cashier_id BEFORE the business_date filter, and excludes category='refund'. Filter chaining order is correct. Test with nonexistent id returns empty (no leaked rows).

Smart-report periods 'yesterday' and 'last_month' use end_dt=23:59:59.999999 / first_of_month - 1µs respectively — correct closed window. 'six_months'=180d, 'year'=365d sliding windows — consistent with existing 'week'(7d) and 'month'(30d).

Both /api/smart-reports/sales (line 12780) and /api/smart-reports/products (line 13567) now share the same period dispatch logic — DRY violation: same 20+ lines duplicated. Extract to a helper _resolve_period_window(period, now) returning (start, end_dt).
Migration POST /api/admin/migrate-business-dates remains idempotent — verified by two consecutive POSTs returning the same response shape with zeros on the second call.

Note: /app/backend/server.py is now 19,683 lines — far above the 700-line guideline. Continued modularisation into routes/* is recommended (already in progress with reports_routes.py, shifts_routes.py, payroll_routes.py).
seed_data_creation: None — read-only test pass for these endpoints. Migration POST was called twice but is idempotent (returns 0 updates after first run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 9 /api/reports/* endpoints + 2 /api/smart-reports/* endpoints work after the business_date refactor and new-period additions. Test file pattern: parametrized over endpoints + over periods. The 4 endpoints (cancellations, discounts, credit, card) require start_date+end_date as mandatory query params — keep this in mind when extending tests; the test handles this via REQUIRES_DATES set. cashier_id filter on /api/reports/expenses verified end-to-end with both existing and nonexistent ids. No bulk-payroll testing was requested in this iteration (HR endpoint not in scope per review_request).
